SEARCH
Package edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.map

Class UnorderedLinkedMap<K,V>

java.lang.Object
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.map.AbstractMap<K,V>
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.map.UnorderedLinkedMap<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - the type of keys stored in the map
V - the type of values that are associated with keys in the map
All Implemented Interfaces:
Map<K,V>, Iterable<K>
public class UnorderedLinkedMap<K,V> extends AbstractMap<K,V>
An unordered link-based map is an unordered (meaning keys are not used to order entries) linked-memory representation of the Map abstract data type. This link-based map delegates to an existing doubly-linked positional list. To help self-organizing entries to improve efficiency of lookUps, the unordered link-based map implements the move-to-front heuristic: each time an entry is accessed, it is shifted to the front of the internal list.
Author:
Dr. King, Diya Patel

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.map.Map

  • Constructor Details

    • UnorderedLinkedMap

      public UnorderedLinkedMap()
      constructs a new positional list made of entries

  • Method Details

    • get

      public V get(K key)
      The code for this method is based on the get algorithm on page 407 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. gets the value at the key
      Parameters:
      key - the key to find the value of
      Returns:
      value the value at the key

    • put

      public V put(K key, V value)
      if the key already exists in the map, update the entry with the given key to have the new value. To facilitate efficent lookUps, apply the move-to-front heuristic on the updated entry, then return the original value that was replaced. Otherwise, if the key does not already exist, insert the new key-value pair at the front of the list (to help facilitate efficient lookUps)
      Parameters:
      key - the key of the entry
      value - the value of the entry
      Returns:
      prevValue the previous value at the position

    • remove

      public V remove(K key)
      removes the value at the key, and if the key doesnt exist it return null
      Parameters:
      key - the key that is to be removed
      Returns:
      the value that was removed

    • size

      public int size()
      returns the size of the list
      Returns:
      size the size of the list

    • entrySet

      public Iterable<Map.Entry<K,V>> entrySet()
      adds entries to an instance of our EntryCollection class
      Returns:
      the collection with all the entries

    • toString

      public String toString()
      returns the string with the entries
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      String made of entries in the list
